task_completion.description: Load both air temperature and SLP datasets into Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply 'Sources' window lists both 'air.mon.ltm.nc' and 'slp.mon.ltm.nc'.

task_completion.description: Create a combined overlay plot of January air temperature and SLP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map with both a color fill (representing air temperature) and contour lines (representing SLP) overlaid on the same map for January.

task_completion.description: Set the map projection of the combined plot to North Polar Stereographic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The combined plot window shows a circular map projection. In the plot settings, 'Stereographic' is selected and centered on Latitude 90 (North Pole).

task_completion.description: Create a 1D line plot of zonally averaged January SLP along the latitude axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply 1D line plot window shows SLP plotted against Latitude. The plot controls should indicate that Longitude is being averaged (zonal mean) and the time is set to January.

task_completion.description: Save both the combined plot and the 1D zonal plot to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing 'Save As' dialogs in Panoply or a terminal/file manager confirming that 'nh_circulation_jan.png' and 'zonal_slp_profile.png' are saved in '~/Documents/WMO_Briefing/'.

task_completion.description: Create a structured diagnostic report with extracted pressure values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of 'circulation_diagnostic.txt' in '~/Documents/WMO_Briefing/'. Look for extracted values: Icelandic Low (~998.6 hPa), Siberian High (~1042.0 hPa), Aleutian Low (~997.6 hPa), and Contrast (~43.4 hPa).

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to create the plots and view data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Panoply application windows are visible in the screenshots being actively used to generate the plots.

integrity.description: The agent inspected the data values within Panoply to extract the pressure values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using the Array tab, data inspector, or hovering over the plot in Panoply to read the specific pressure values for the required regions.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the diagnostic report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or constructing the report in a text editor or terminal, rather than copying a pre-existing completed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis long-term mean climatology data. January is time index 0. The SLP variable is stored in millibars (equivalent to hPa), so no division by 100 is needed. Verified January SLP values: Icelandic Low at (65.0N, 340.0E) = 998.59 hPa, Siberian High at (50.0N, 90.0E) = 1041.99 hPa, Aleutian Low at (50.0N, 180.0E) = 997.59 hPa. The pressure contrast (Siberian minus Icelandic) = 43.40 hPa. The Siberian High is the global January SLP maximum, making Siberia the DOMINANT_HIGH_REGION. The combined plot must be a North Polar Stereographic projection centered at 90N. The 1D plot must be a zonal mean of SLP along the latitude axis.
